question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with each of the following words:

Vindictive, Deteriorate, Ambiguity, Narrative, Contemplate
solution_html:
Vindictive: Her tone was cold and vindictive, hinting she hadn’t forgiven him.

Deteriorate: Without regular maintenance, the building began to deteriorate quickly.

Ambiguity: The poem’s ambiguity allows for multiple interpretations.

Narrative: The book follows a nonlinear narrative structure that challenges the reader.

Contemplate: She sat in silence to contemplate the deeper questions of life.

Marks Distribution:

For making meaningful correct sentences; (0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with appropriate words:

Technology has become an (a) ______ part of modern existence, seamlessly embedded into the way we work, connect, and live. It shapes how we communicate, learn, and (b) ______ daily functions, offering tools that once existed only in fiction. When harnessed thoughtfully, it (c) ______ efficiency, stimulates creativity, and propels global (d) ______ across industries and societies. Yet, as empowering as it is, technology is not without its shadows; its misuse can deepen social (e) ______, create ethical dilemmas, and challenge our sense of privacy and control.
solution_html:
(a) integral / essential / inseparable / important / indispensable

(b) operate / navigate / manage / coordinate / support / facilitate / streamline

(c) enhances / increases / accelerates / boosts / optimizes / elevates / improves / refines / augments

(d) progress/ development/ advancement/ transformation/ innovation/ growth

(e) divides/ gaps/ barriers/ distances/ divisions/ inequalities/ rifts/ disparities/ fragmentation

Marks Distribution:

For filling gaps with appropriate words; (0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Traditional Education vs Online Education’.
solution_html:
Traditional Education vs Online Education

In today’s fast-changing world, education no longer belongs only within four classroom walls. Traditional education, with its structured schedule and face-to-face interaction, offers discipline, social skills, and immediate feedback from teachers. It encourages group activities and real-time discussions that help develop communication and collaboration. In contrast, online education brings flexibility, allowing learners to study anytime, anywhere. It provides access to global resources and self-paced learning, making it ideal for working students or those in remote areas. However, it often lacks personal interaction and may lead to distractions or lack of motivation. Both systems have their advantages and drawbacks, depending on the learner’s needs, goals, and environment. The best approach may lie in blending both methods — using technology without losing the human touch. As education evolves, one thing is clear: how we learn may change, but the desire to learn must always stay alive.

Mark distribution:

For writing Title ‘Traditional Education vs Online Education’; 0.5 mark.

For writing clear explanation/definition of traditional education and online education; 02 marks.

For writing key points about traditional education and online education; 02 marks.

For writing final thought about traditional education and online education; 0.5 mark.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.
